title: Preparing cloth for marbling
sources: Josef Halfer, The Progress of the Marbling Art (1893, public domain) — marbling on fabric
notes: Natural-fibre fabrics (cotton, silk, linen) marble well when mordanted with alum — the same preparation as paper. The cloth must be wetted, sponged with the alum solution, dried flat, and used promptly before the mordant loses activity. Covers: mordant concentration for cloth vs paper, preventing the cloth from sinking into the size, lifting and rinsing. Marbled cloth is used for bookbinding, scrapbooking, and framing.
